data.title: Environmentalists sue to stop BP ultra-deepwater project in U.S. Gulf
data.description:
Environmentalists have sued to reverse the Trump administration’s approval of BP's (BP) $5B Kaskida ultra-deepwater drilling project in the Gulf of Mexico, alleging the proposed project threatens the region's ecosystems and endangered species.

Groups including the Center for Biological Diversity, Sierra Club, and Healthy Gulf said in the lawsuit filed with the 11th Circuit U.S. Court of Appeals that the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management approved a plan with faulty estimates for a worst-case oil spill from the project, which is expected to produce 80K bbl/day of oil.

BP's (BP) proposal lacked required information and does not demonstrate that it can safely drill as far as six miles below the sea floor, the lawsuit said, noting the drilling depth for the Kaskida project will be greater than that of Deepwater Horizon, which exploded in 2010 and led to the biggest oil spill in U.S. history.

BP (BP) said the lawsuit was "unfounded" and that it “singles out BP in an apparent broader effort to block not only the Kaskida project but all future offshore oil and gas development in the U.S."

The Kaskida platform represents "a major step forward," the U.S. Interior Department said, unlocking more than 275M barrels of previously unrecoverable oil in the Gulf.

data.title: Uber discloses 11.52% stake in Lucid
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER) disclosed an 11.52% passive stake in Lucid (LCID), representing over 37.75M shares.

The shares are held through SMB Holding, an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of Uber, according to a regulatory filing.

Shares of Newark, California-based Lucid Group were up over 1% in extended trading on Monday.

Earlier on April 14, Lucid priced a $300M public offering of common stock, part of a larger $1.05B capital raise that includes investments from Uber and Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund.

As part of the pact, Uber (UBER) said it will increase its purchase commitment to at least 35,000 Lucid vehicles designed exclusively for use as part of Uber's future global robotaxi service. Uber has also committed to an additional $200M investment in Lucid, raising Uber's total investments to $500M to date.

data.title: Gold and silver slide on renewed Middle East war tensions
data.description:
Gold and silver futures fell Monday as ​the dollar and oil ​prices rose following Iran's threat of retaliation in response to the seizure of an Iranian cargo vessel by U.S. forces, dimming hopes of a breakthrough in efforts to end the Middle East war.

Iran wavered on whether to send diplomats to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks after the U.S. seized the ship and maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

The U.S. dollar rose to its highest level in a week before paring gains to trade up ‌0.1%, and benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yields gained, increasing ​the opportunity cost of holding non-yielding bullion.

"The latest weakness has been driven by renewed dollar strength and fresh concerns about energy-led inflation," Saxo Bank analysts said in a note. "Gold and silver remain highly sensitive to developments in the Middle East given the knock-on impact on the dollar, bond yields, and U.S. rate expectations."

Meanwhile, gold demand during one of India's key buying festivals remained subdued on Sunday as record prices ​curbed jewelry purchases, offsetting a ‌modest uptick in investment demand.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed 1% lower at $4,806.60/oz, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled down 2.2% to $79.951/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Snap appoints insider Doug Hott as new CFO
data.description:
Snap (SNAP) said it has appointed insider Doug Hott as its new Chief Financial Officer.

The social media company’s current CFO, Derek Andersen, will depart, effective May 8, 2026, according to an SEC filing.

Hott, 53, is the current Vice President of Finance, Strategy, and Corporate Development in the company since July 2024. Before that, Hott served as Snap’s Vice President of Finance.

The company's stock was trading 0.8% lower after-hours on Monday.

data.title: RTX's unit wins $213M contract modification for Zumwalt systems upgrade
data.description:
• Raytheon Missiles and Defense, a unit of RTX (RTX), won a $213.4M contract modification on Monday for Zumwalt-class Navy combat systems.
  • The contract covers installation, integration, testing, maintenance, and upgrades of mission systems.
  • The work is expected to be completed by April 2027.
  • The FY25 and FY26 Navy budget funds will be obligated at the time of award, of which ~$32.3M will expire by the end of FY26.
  • The contract is managed by Naval Sea Systems Command.
  • The stock traded slightly higher at $195.96 in the after-hours trading session.

data.title: Westlake Chemical Partners LP names new CFO
data.description:
• Westlake Chemical Partners (WKL) appointed Jonathan H. Baksht as senior vice president, chief financial officer, and director effective June 15, 2026.
  • Baksht will succeed M. Steven Bender, who will retire and transition to special advisor.
  • Bender will also step down from the board of the general partner on the same date.
  • Baksht most recently served as CFO of Fortune Brands Innovations.
